items.briefing:
The Australian Cyber Security Centre (ACSC) is warning that login credentials for Fortinet firewalls and VPN gateways have been exposed. If your organisation uses these products, IT staff should check for unauthorised access and update passwords as a priority. Separately, a Cloudflare report highlights that cyberattacks on civil society organisations — including Australian groups — are increasing, meaning charities, advocacy groups, and non-profits should not assume they are low-value targets.

An IBM study also flags that many executives are uncertain about who controls their AI systems and data, which is a growing risk for organisations adopting AI tools. If your organisation uses AI services, ask your provider where your data is stored and who can access it.

**Practical tip:** If your organisation uses Fortinet VPN or firewall products, ask your IT team today to confirm whether your credentials may be affected and whether multi-factor authentication is enabled.

featured.title: ACSC warns of reported credential exposure affecting Fortinet firewalls and VPN gateways
featured.quote: ACSC warns of widespread credential exposure affecting critical infrastructure like Fortinet firewalls.
